behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this behavior is a static lint rule over prompt-template text. PASS2-AUDIT: challenged Integration/System/Agentic. Integration rejected: behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," so failure is local rule logic, not module handoff. System rejected: no required real OS/network/subprocess boundary. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model call or multi-step agent pipeline in this behavior. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template fixtures: one containing copied phase instructions and one clean template. Assert leaked template is rejected with precise violation spans/rule id, and clean template passes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because leakage may appear only after interpolation + fixture composition across modules. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected due behavior text "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness," which is explicitly cross-module wiring. System rejected: no real network/subprocess needed; in-process harness assembly is enough. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no dependence on model output. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt builder pipeline (template + interpolation + fixtures) and assert the final assembled prompt has no forbidden phase-instruction segments from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is module interaction between invocation flow, skill loader, and prompt/validator components. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected since failure is at handoff boundary (loaded file content vs hard-coded fallback). System rejected: local filesystem and in-process modules suffice; no required real HTTP/subprocess. Agentic rejected because behavior is source-of-truth wiring, not model-quality judgment. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation with a temporary plugin/skill directory containing known skill text, and assert the run uses loaded file content (and not hard-coded phase rules) by checking constructed context/metadata used by validator.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, focused on harness configurability and variant switching. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because variant selection and prompt-run orchestration cross components. System rejected: local variant files/fixtures are enough; no real external infra required. Agentic considered but rejected for this behavior text: it checks ability to run "without changing test prompt text," not correctness of model decisions. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness executions (baseline skill vs degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical user prompt text and assert prompt text hash is unchanged while selected skill variant differs.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because pass/fail depends on how the real model behavior changes when skill guidance regresses. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected by REJECTION-GATE: behavior text requires "behavioral test fails" under same prompt after skill degradation, which is exactly model-output dependence; stubbed outputs could hide this. Workflow considered, rejected because only a single model-behavior decision boundary is required, not multiple agentic stages. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test twice with identical user prompt: baseline SKILL.md and degraded SKILL.md section removed. Assert baseline passes structural behavior checks and degraded variant fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this is ordering across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser modules. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit considered but rejected because sequencing bug often appears at module handoff. System rejected: local filesystem artifact is sufficient; no real OS primitive beyond normal file write semantics needed. Agentic rejected: can use deterministic mock output; correctness is ordering, not model judgment quality. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness with a controlled malformed response payload; assert raw output artifact is written first and preserved even when downstream JSON parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this is assertion-engine logic. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected; behavior text targets constraint rules themselves, which can be validated with crafted payloads in one module. System rejected: no real infra required. Agentic rejected: no need for real model output to prove structural-vs-keyword assertion semantics. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the assertion function crafted outputs where keywords are present but schema path/role placement is wrong; assert failure. Feed structurally correct output lacking keywords; assert expected structural decision per rule set.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic due explicit "live model execution" and regression detection based on real generated structure. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected by behavior text: mocked deterministic output cannot validate miss-rate gap between structural and keyword-only checks under real model variation. Workflow considered but rejected because requirement is one live generation + two assertion strategies, not multi-agentic pipeline stages. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model test cases, evaluate outputs with both keyword-only and structural assertion sets, and assert at least one regression is detected only by structural checks.
